original_query: select  	s_store_name, 	i_item_desc, 	sc.revenue, 	i_current_price, 	i_wholesale_cost, 	i_brand  from store, item,      (select ss_store_sk, avg(revenue) as ave  	from  	    (select  ss_store_sk, ss_item_sk,  		     sum(ss_sales_price) as revenue  		from store_sales, date_dim  		where ss_sold_date_sk = d_date_sk and d_month_seq between 1192 and 1192+11     and ss_sales_price / ss_list_price BETWEEN 68 * 0.01 AND 78 * 0.01  		group by ss_store_sk, ss_item_sk) sa  	group by ss_store_sk) sb,      (select  ss_store_sk, ss_item_sk, sum(ss_sales_price) as revenue  	from store_sales, date_dim  	where ss_sold_date_sk = d_date_sk and d_month_seq between 1192 and 1192+11   and ss_sales_price / ss_list_price BETWEEN 68 * 0.01 AND 78 * 0.01  	group by ss_store_sk, ss_item_sk) sc  where sb.ss_store_sk = sc.ss_store_sk and        sc.revenue <= 0.1 * sb.ave and        s_store_sk = sc.ss_store_sk and        i_item_sk = sc.ss_item_sk        and i_manager_id BETWEEN 79 and 83        and s_state in ('IN','NM','VA')  order by s_store_name, i_item_desc limit 100;
rewritten_query:
WITH store_item_revenue AS (
    SELECT 
        ss_store_sk, 
        ss_item_sk, 
        SUM(ss_sales_price) AS revenue,
        AVG(SUM(ss_sales_price)) OVER (PARTITION BY ss_store_sk) AS avg_revenue_per_store
    FROM store_sales
    JOIN date_dim ON ss_sold_date_sk = d_date_sk
    WHERE d_month_seq BETWEEN 1192 AND 1192 + 11
      AND ss_sales_price BETWEEN 0.68 * ss_list_price AND 0.78 * ss_list_price
    GROUP BY ss_store_sk, ss_item_sk
)
SELECT 
    s.s_store_name,
    i.i_item_desc,
    sir.revenue,
    i.i_current_price,
    i.i_wholesale_cost,
    i.i_brand
FROM store_item_revenue sir
JOIN store s ON sir.ss_store_sk = s.s_store_sk
JOIN item i ON sir.ss_item_sk = i.i_item_sk
WHERE sir.revenue <= 0.1 * sir.avg_revenue_per_store
  AND i.i_manager_id BETWEEN 79 AND 83
  AND s.s_state IN ('IN', 'NM', 'VA')
ORDER BY s.s_store_name, i.i_item_desc
LIMIT 100;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Eliminate redundant subqueries by using a CTE with a window function. The original query calculates the same subquery twice (for sa and sc).
Combine the sb and sc subqueries into a single CTE to avoid a merge join between them.
Replace division with multiplication in the filter condition (ss_sales_price / ss_list_price) which is more computationally expensive.
